product.name: ReasonDB
product.description: A reasoning-native database for AI agent workflows with LLM-guided tree traversal
product.targetAudience: External developers integrating ReasonDB into AI applications
keyFeatures: RQL query language with SEARCH and REASON clauses
Multi-provider LLM support (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Cohere)

glossary.node: A single unit in the document tree, containing content and an LLM-generated summary
glossary.tree: The hierarchical structure of a document, with nodes as branches and leaves
glossary.beam search: The parallel exploration algorithm used for tree traversal
glossary.RQL: ReasonDB Query Language - SQL-like syntax with SEARCH and REASON clauses
glossary.SEARCH clause: BM25 full-text keyword search in RQL
glossary.REASON clause: LLM semantic search with answer extraction in RQL
glossary.table: A collection that organizes documents into logical groups

authentication.description: No authentication required for local development

conventions.pagination: LIMIT and OFFSET in RQL queries

commonPatterns: Use table_id in all ingestion requests
Use /search for LLM reasoning, /query for filtering

keyDirectories.core: crates/reasondb-core/ - Core library (models, storage, LLM engine)

codeExamples.style: Practical, working examples with realistic data

insights: ReasonDB differentiates from vector DBs by using LLM reasoning instead of embeddings
Documents must be assigned to tables during ingestion (table_id required)
SEARCH clause uses BM25 for fast keyword matching, REASON uses LLM for semantic understanding
